notes: Sample-only queue for the community AI architecture. No live AI calls, no API keys, and no auto-approval are represented here.

requests.candidate_summary: Propose a candidate sawmill label for the first Texarkana building anchor and keep it pending until a human reviews the Sanborn evidence.
requests.provenance_notes: Candidate-only label suggestion anchored to the 1885 Sanborn source; no verified building identity is claimed.

requests.candidate_summary: Propose a candidate railroad depot label for the second building anchor and keep the result pending review.
requests.provenance_notes: Candidate-only label suggestion from the Sanborn map context; this is not a verified identity claim.

requests.candidate_summary: Keep the OCR-based person match in review until a human confirms the identity and any location link.
requests.provenance_notes: Source issue OCR is the evidence anchor; identity remains candidate_pending_review.

requests.candidate_summary: Keep the business match tied to the issue page until a reviewer confirms the source trail and identity.
requests.provenance_notes: Business identity is source-linked and must stay in review until confirmed.

requests.candidate_label: Daily Texarkana Democrat page 3 source link
requests.candidate_summary: Preserve the issue-page source link so downstream people and business review can cite the same evidence trail.
requests.provenance_notes: Source link candidate only; no historical promotion or auto-approval.

requests.style: dark historical brass/parchment illustration with sepia linework and a transparent cutout silhouette
requests.provenance_notes: Illustrative building art anchored to the reviewed 1885 Sanborn source. It does not assert a verified sawmill identity.
requests.notes: Fit the asset to the footprint and keep the map geometry separate.

requests.style: dark historical brass/parchment illustration with industrial detailing and transparent cutout edges
requests.provenance_notes: Illustrative building art anchored to the reviewed cotton seed oil mill record. The image remains illustrative until separately reviewed.
requests.notes: Keep the industrial mass within the reviewed footprint.

requests.style: dark historical brass/parchment illustration with railroad architecture cues and transparent cutout edges
requests.provenance_notes: Illustrative depot asset for a candidate building shell; it is not a verified railroad depot claim.
requests.notes: Keep the asset compatible with later footprint review.

requests.style: dark historical brass/parchment illustration with stable-yard detail and transparent cutout edges
requests.provenance_notes: Illustrative art anchored to the reviewed A.S. Blythe wagon yard and livery record. The art remains illustrative until reviewed separately.
requests.notes: Preserve the reviewed label alignment with the footprint.

requests.style: aged parchment panel texture with grain, fold marks, brass wear, and dark ink wash edges
requests.provenance_notes: Interface texture is illustrative and uses the historical paper language of the community shell. It does not add new historical claims.
requests.notes: Use as a reusable shell texture rather than a map cutout.

requests.style: weathered dirt road texture with muted earth tones and subtle map-paper grain
requests.provenance_notes: Illustrative road texture for the map layer stack; it remains separate from the reviewed source geometry.
requests.notes: Keep the texture tileable and compatible with the road/rail layer.

requests.style: historical grass and brush texture with sparse vegetation, sepia wear, and map-paper grain
requests.provenance_notes: Illustrative terrain texture for the base map layer. It is not a claim about exact vegetation coverage.
requests.notes: Keep the terrain readable beneath buildings and labels.

requests.style: aged rail line texture with iron-dark rails, ties, and weathered map-paper edges
requests.provenance_notes: Illustrative rail texture for the road/rail layer. It stays separate from stitched geometry and control points.
requests.notes: Use as a reusable line texture rather than a finalized alignment output.
